---
title: "Weather tool"
subtitle: "Demo for deck 04: Beyond prompts"
format: html
---

Take an ordinary function that fetches a weather forecast, turn it into a tool,
and let Claude call it on demand. This is the "automatic" tool-calling loop: the
model decides to call the tool, and `chatlas` runs it and feeds the result back.

Run the chunks interactively. Needs `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` in your environment and
the `NWS` package for forecasts.

## A plain weather function

We can already get a forecast with the `NWS` package, no LLM involved.

```{python}
import NWS

provo = {"lat": "40.2338", "lon": "-111.6585"}

# Initiate the NWS API
NWS.InitiateAPI("SIAS 2026", "julia.silge@posit.co")

# Forecast for the SIAS 2026 location
forecast = NWS.GetCurrentForecast(provo["lat"], provo["lon"])
forecast
```

## Turn it into a tool

A tool is just a function with a clear docstring describing what it does and its
arguments. `chatlas` reads the docstring to build the schema the model sees.

```{python}
def get_weather(lat: float, lon: float):
    """
    Get forecast data for a specific latitude and longitude.

    Parameters
    ----------
    lat : str
        Latitude of the location.
    lon : str
        Longitude of the location.
    """
    return NWS.GetCurrentForecast(lat, lon)
```

It is still an ordinary callable:

```{python}
get_weather(provo["lat"], provo["lon"])
```

## Let the model call it

Register the tool on a Claude chat, then ask a question. When the model needs the
weather it calls `get_weather` itself, and `chatlas` handles the round trip.

```{python}
import chatlas

chat = chatlas.ChatAnthropic()
chat.register_tool(get_weather)

chat.chat("What should I wear to SIAS 2026 in Provo, UT?")
```
